title: Wing Skin Crack Repair — Category A
procedure: Stop all flight operations. Inspect crack boundaries using dye penetrant method. Apply temporary crack arrest hole at crack tip (3mm diameter). Contact Boeing MRO engineering for permanent repair authorization. Document in aircraft logbook under ATA Chapter 57.

title: Wing Skin Crack Repair — Category A
procedure: Stop all flight operations. Inspect crack boundaries using dye penetrant method. Apply temporary crack arrest hole at crack tip (3mm diameter). Contact Boeing MRO engineering for permanent repair authorization. Document in aircraft logbook under ATA Chapter 57.

title: Fuselage Skin Crack Repair
repair_category: Category B — Action Within 10 Flight Cycles
procedure: Mark crack boundaries with felt tip. Measure crack length using calibrated crack gauge. If under 3mm — monitor and re-inspect at next scheduled check. If over 3mm — ground aircraft and raise maintenance order. Document under ATA Chapter 53.

title: Rear Fuselage Crack Inspection
repair_category: Category B — Action Within 10 Flight Cycles
procedure: Mark crack boundaries with felt tip. Measure crack length using calibrated crack gauge. If under 3mm — monitor and re-inspect at next scheduled check. If over 3mm — ground aircraft and raise maintenance order. Document under ATA Chapter 53.

title: Nose Section Crack Repair — Critical Zone
procedure: Ground aircraft immediately. Nose section is a critical structural zone. Perform full eddy current inspection of surrounding 150mm area. Raise urgent maintenance order. No flight operations until cleared by Boeing MRO engineering.

title: Tail Section Crack Repair
procedure: Ground aircraft. Tail section cracks affect structural integrity of empennage. Perform visual and tactile inspection of full tail cone. Check horizontal and vertical stabilizer attachment points. Raise urgent maintenance order.

title: Fuselage Corrosion Treatment
repair_category: Category C — Action Within 50 Flight Cycles
procedure: Clean affected area with approved solvent (Skydrol-safe). Remove corrosion products using scotch-brite pad. Apply corrosion inhibiting compound (CIC) — Henkel Alodine 1200. Re-prime with epoxy primer within 4 hours. Document under ATA Chapter 53.
ad_reference: DGCA CAR Section 2 Series F Part I

title: Wing Corrosion Treatment
repair_category: Category C — Action Within 50 Flight Cycles
procedure: Clean affected area with approved solvent. Remove corrosion using scotch-brite — do not use abrasive tools on wing skin. Apply Alodine 1200. Re-prime and topcoat within 8 hours. If corrosion depth exceeds 0.5mm, contact Boeing structural engineering.
ad_reference: DGCA CAR Section 2 Series F Part I

title: Fuselage Dent Assessment
repair_category: Category B — Action Within 10 Flight Cycles
procedure: Measure dent depth using depth gauge. Measure dent diameter. If depth-to-diameter ratio under 0.05 — cosmetic only, no structural repair needed. If ratio over 0.05 — raise maintenance order for structural assessment. Document dimensions in logbook.

title: CFRP Delamination Inspection
procedure: Do not probe delamination with sharp tools — risk of propagation. Perform tap test over 200mm surrounding area. Mark delamination boundary with chalk. Photograph and measure. Ground aircraft if delamination exceeds 50mm diameter or is within 25mm of fastener. Raise urgent maintenance order.

title: Paint Blister Treatment
repair_category: Category D — Action At Next Scheduled Check
procedure: Inspect underlying surface for corrosion after removing blister. If no corrosion — sand, prime, and repaint at next C-check. If corrosion found — escalate to AMM-007 corrosion procedure. Document in cosmetic defect log.
